extra.message: Unsanitized input from the HTTP request body flows into save, where it is used as a path. This may result in a Path Traversal vulnerability and allow an attacker to write arbitrary files.
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into os.remove, where it is used as a path. This may result in a Path Traversal vulnerability and allow an attacker to remove arbitrary files.
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into execute, where it is used in an SQL query. This may result in an SQL Injection vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from the HTTP request body flows into execute, where it is used in an SQL query. This may result in an SQL Injection vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from the request URL flows into flask.redirect, where it is used as input for request redirection. This may result in an Open Redirect vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from the request URL flows into flask.redirect, where it is used as input for request redirection. This may result in an Open Redirect vulnerability.
hashlib.md5 is insecure. Consider changing it to a secure hashing algorithm.
hashlib.md5 is insecure. Consider changing it to a secure hashing algorithm.
